LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEFENDANTS ST. LUKE'S HOSPITAL, A CALIF CORP AND SUTTER HEALTH, A CALIF CORP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT--OFF CALENDAR, FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED ON 8/14. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558.

LAW AND MOTION 301,NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS ST. LUKE'S HOSPITAL, A CALIF CORP AND SUTTER HEALTH, A CALIF CORP'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT -- 1) DEMURRER SUSTAINED ON 1ST, 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING JOINT EMPLOYER RELATIONSHIP. 2) DEMURRER SUSTAINED AS TO 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LIBEL WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING JOINT EMPLOYER RELATIONSHIP AS TO DEFENDANT SUTTER AND PUBLICATION AS TO DEFENDANT ST. LUKES'. 3) DEMURRER SUSTAINED AS TO 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE NEGLIGENT SUPERVISION CLAIM CANNOT STEM FROM WRONGFUL TERMINATION CLAIM UNDER HINES. REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN, CSR #5220

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2004 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
RULING:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT ST. LUKE'S HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA CORP. and SUTTER HEALTH'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT: 1) SUSTAIN all demurrers without leave to amend as to Sutter; 2) St. Luke has 10 days leave to answer. Prevailing party to submit form of order which shall also address notice. (301/JLW/AC) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2004 Continued To Jan-15-04 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
RULING:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. DEFENDANT ST. LUKE'S HOSPITAL, A CALIF CORP. and SUTTER HEALTH, A CALIF CORP.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT: CONTINUED on the Court's own motion until 01/15/04. Plaintiff is to provide courtesy copies of the opposition per Local Rule 8.5. (301/JLW/AC) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-22-2003 Continued To Feb-13-04 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-04-2003 Continued To Jan-02-04 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-14-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTs ST. LUKE'S HOSPITAL, A CALIF CORP and SUTTER HEALTH, A CALIF CORP'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT -- 1) Demurrer sustained on 1st, 2nd and 3rd causes of action with 10 days leave to amend to allege facts supporting joint employer relationship. 2) Demurrer sustained as to 5th cause of action for libel with 10 days leave to amend to allege facts supporting joint employer relationship as to defendant Sutter and publication as to defendant St. Lukes'. 3) Demurrer sustained as to 4th cause of action without leave to amend because negligent supervision claim cannot stem from wrongful termination claim under Hines. (301/PHA/AC) Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR #5220

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
